This module acts as the centralized source of truth for all application con
facts.lesson: Use Final type hints for configuration constants to prevent accidental runtime mutations and improve IDE static analysis accuracy.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppConfig (src/config.py)
This module acts as the centralized source of truth for all application con
facts.lesson: Centralize configuration in a dedicated module to ensure a single source of truth and simplify environment variable management across the application.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppConfig (src/config.py)
This module acts as the centralized source of truth for all application con
facts.lesson: Provide sensible, safe defaults for UI constants like dimensions and paths to prevent application crashes when environment variables are missing.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskModel (src/models/task.py)
Defines the core data schema for a task entity within the application.
facts.lesson: Use Python dataclasses with explicit type hinting for internal data models to ensure runtime type safety and improve IDE autocompletion for service layer developers.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskModel (src/models/task.py)
Defines the core data schema for a task entity within the application.
facts.lesson: Implement symmetrical to_dict/from_dict serialization helpers within domain models to decouple internal business logic from persistence layer formats.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskInputView (src/ui/components/input_view.py)
Provides a user interface widget for accepting input
facts.lesson: Decouple UI components from business logic by using callback functions instead of direct method calls to ensure framework-agnostic component design.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskInputView (src/ui/components/input_view.py)
Provides a user interface widget for accepting input
facts.lesson: Validate user input at the component level using guard clauses before triggering submission events to prevent downstream logic errors and minimize state corruption.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TaskInputView (src/ui/components/input_view.py)
Provides a user interface widget for accepting input
facts.lesson: Encapsulate state mutation (like clearing text fields) within public methods to maintain strict control over internal component data flow.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement EventBus (src/utils/event_bus.py)
A centralized messaging component that implements the Observer patt
facts.lesson: Use reentrant locks (threading.RLock) instead of standard locks when implementing observers to prevent deadlocks if a callback attempts to emit further events or modify the subscription list.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement EventBus (src/utils/event_bus.py)
A centralized messaging component that implements the Observer patt
facts.lesson: Favor weak references (weakref) for subscriber callbacks to prevent memory leaks in long-running UI applications where components are frequently created and destroyed.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement ThemeManager (src/utils/theme.py)
Provides utility functions to manage and apply application-wide sty
facts.lesson: Decouple GUI styling logic from business logic by centralizing property application in a dedicated ThemeManager class that iterates through widget trees.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement ThemeManager (src/utils/theme.py)
Provides utility functions to manage and apply application-wide sty
facts.lesson: Inject configuration through a centralized AppConfig pattern rather than hardcoding style values to ensure dynamic theme switching and simplified environment-specific styling.

Handles all CRUD operations for task entities, abstracting th
facts.lesson: Use dataclasses.asdict for lightweight persistence serialization instead of manual key-value mapping to ensure schema consistency between internal models and storage layers.

Handles all CRUD operations for task entities, abstracting th
facts.lesson: Decouple business logic from persistence by enforcing a repository pattern that hides the storage backend (e.g., JSON vs SQL), facilitating future migration without changing service-layer code.

Handles all CRUD operations for task entities, abstracting th
facts.lesson: Implement granular file locking when using JSON-based repositories to prevent race conditions during concurrent CRUD operations in multi-process or multi-threaded environments.

Contains the business logic layer that orchestrates intera

PHASE 1: Implement TaskService (src/services/task_service.py)
Contains the business logic layer that orchestrates intera
facts.lesson: Favor composition over inheritance when orchestrating multiple services to keep the orchestration layer modular and prevent deep class hierarchies.

Defines the primary graphical container and top-level structure of the

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppController (src/controller.py)
Acts as the central coordinator between the UI layer and the busine
facts.lesson: Decouple UI and business logic by using an EventBus within the AppController; this prevents tight coupling and ensures components remain independently testable.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppController (src/controller.py)
Acts as the central coordinator between the UI layer and the busine
facts.lesson: Inject dependencies into controllers via constructor arguments rather than global singletons to facilitate mock-based unit testing of coordination logic.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppController (src/controller.py)
Acts as the central coordinator between the UI layer and the busine
facts.lesson: Implement structured logging at controller entry points to trace user-triggered events through the system, simplifying debugging of orchestration flows.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MainWindow (src/ui/main_window.py)
The top-level window class that initializes the application UI env
facts.lesson: Utilize Dependency Injection in UI constructors to decouple the MainWindow from concrete service implementations, facilitating easier unit testing and modular component replacement.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MainWindow (src/ui/main_window.py)
The top-level window class that initializes the application UI env
facts.lesson: Enforce explicit lifecycle management by providing a standardized 'run' or 'start' method in the top-level window class to ensure proper initialization of the EventBus and backend services.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MainWindow (src/ui/main_window.py)
The top-level window class that initializes the application UI env
facts.lesson: Define clear interface contracts for UI components (e.g., MainLayout) to avoid tight coupling between the UI logic and data repository layers, ensuring future-proof integration.

PHASE 1: Implement IntegrationTester (tests/integration.py)
Defines the suite of automated tests for verifying full end-
facts.lesson: Implement idempotent teardown logic in integration tests using a try-finally block to ensure local test artifacts are purged regardless of whether the test suite succeeds or crashes.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement IntegrationTester (tests/integration.py)
Defines the suite of automated tests for verifying full end-
facts.lesson: Decouple storage layers from business services using dependency injection to facilitate testing against ephemeral in-memory or temporary file backends without mutating production state.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement IntegrationTester (tests/integration.py)
Defines the suite of automated tests for verifying full end-
facts.lesson: Centralize test execution in an entry-point class to standardize environment setup and teardown, preventing configuration drift across heterogeneous test modules.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TestRunner (tests/runner.py)
Provides the primary entry point for executing the application's test su
facts.lesson: Utilize unittest.TestLoader().discover() for automated test suite scaling; avoid manual list maintenance to ensure new test files are automatically included in the execution cycle.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TestRunner (tests/runner.py)
Provides the primary entry point for executing the application's test su
facts.lesson: Implement failure reporting via standard logging modules rather than print statements to preserve CI/CD pipeline readability and facilitate automated failure analysis.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement TestRunner (tests/runner.py)
Provides the primary entry point for executing the application's test su
facts.lesson: Enforce consistent execution interfaces by wrapping test runners in a unified class structure, decoupling the internal testing framework (e.g., unittest) from the application deployment lifecycle.

nodes.lesson: Use Final type hints for configuration constants to prevent accidental runtime mutations and improve IDE static analysis accuracy.
Centralize configuration in a dedicated module to ensure a single source of truth and simplify environment variable management across the application.
Provide sensible, safe defaults for UI constants like dimensions and paths to prevent application crashes when environment variables are missing.
Use Python dataclasses with explicit type hinting for internal data models to ensure runtime type safety and improve IDE autocompletion for service layer developers.
Implement symmetrical to_dict/from_dict serialization helpers within domain models to decouple internal business logic from persistence layer formats.
Decouple UI components from business logic by using callback functions instead of direct method calls to ensure framework-agnostic component design.
Validate user input at the component level using guard clauses before triggering submission events to prevent downstream logic errors and minimize state corruption.
Encapsulate state mutation (like clearing text fields) within public methods to maintain strict control over internal component data flow.
Use reentrant locks (threading.RLock) instead of standard locks when implementing observers to prevent deadlocks if a callback attempts to emit further events or modify the subscription list.
Favor weak references (weakref) for subscriber callbacks to prevent memory leaks in long-running UI applications where components are frequently created and destroyed.
Decouple GUI styling logic from business logic by centralizing property application in a dedicated ThemeManager class that iterates through widget trees.
Inject configuration through a centralized AppConfig pattern rather than hardcoding style values to ensure dynamic theme switching and simplified environment-specific styling.
Use dataclasses.asdict for lightweight persistence serialization instead of manual key-value mapping to ensure schema consistency between internal models and storage layers.
Decouple business logic from persistence by enforcing a repository pattern that hides the storage backend (e.g., JSON vs SQL), facilitating future migration without changing service-layer code.
Implement granular file locking when using JSON-based repositories to prevent race conditions during concurrent CRUD operations in multi-process or multi-threaded environments.
In tkinter.ttk.Treeview, store sensitive metadata like database IDs in hidden columns rather than relying on visible text, ensuring UI updates don't break data retrieval logic.
Implement decoupled interaction handlers using callback patterns in UI components to isolate business logic from presentation layer code.
Decouple business logic from persistence by requiring repositories to implement a strict interface, ensuring services remain agnostic of the underlying database technology.
Implement explicit data validation at the service boundary before passing DTOs to repositories to prevent malformed data from corrupting the persistent state.
Favor composition over inheritance when orchestrating multiple services to keep the orchestration layer modular and prevent deep class hierarchies.
Avoid tight coupling between UI components and unstable config interfaces by using placeholder mocks or dependency injection patterns until the internal contract stabilizes.
Implement automated smoke tests using minimal instantiation scripts to verify structural integrity of UI layouts before full integration.
Decouple UI and business logic by using an EventBus within the AppController; this prevents tight coupling and ensures components remain independently testable.
Inject dependencies into controllers via constructor arguments rather than global singletons to facilitate mock-based unit testing of coordination logic.
Implement structured logging at controller entry points to trace user-triggered events through the system, simplifying debugging of orchestration flows.
Utilize Dependency Injection in UI constructors to decouple the MainWindow from concrete service implementations, facilitating easier unit testing and modular component replacement.
Enforce explicit lifecycle management by providing a standardized 'run' or 'start' method in the top-level window class to ensure proper initialization of the EventBus and backend services.
Define clear interface contracts for UI components (e.g., MainLayout) to avoid tight coupling between the UI logic and data repository layers, ensuring future-proof integration.
Implement idempotent teardown logic in integration tests using a try-finally block to ensure local test artifacts are purged regardless of whether the test suite succeeds or crashes.
Decouple storage layers from business services using dependency injection to facilitate testing against ephemeral in-memory or temporary file backends without mutating production state.
Centralize test execution in an entry-point class to standardize environment setup and teardown, preventing configuration drift across heterogeneous test modules.
Utilize unittest.TestLoader().discover() for automated test suite scaling; avoid manual list maintenance to ensure new test files are automatically included in the execution cycle.
Implement failure reporting via standard logging modules rather than print statements to preserve CI/CD pipeline readability and facilitate automated failure analysis.
Enforce consistent execution interfaces by wrapping test runners in a unified class structure, decoupling the internal testing framework (e.g., unittest) from the application deployment lifecycle.
